primary_decisions_supported: Customer success focus areas by segment
executive_questions_answered: How are product adoption rates differing across customer segments, and how does that correlate with upsell/expansion?
Which segments or cohorts have the fastest (or slowest) time-to-value and feature adoption?
Are segments with higher product adoption seeing higher expansion or retention?

metric_definitions.definition: Percentage of customers (or users) who have adopted a key product feature or reached a defined engagement threshold, segmented by customer category.
metric_definitions.formula: Adoption% = (Number of accounts in segment using feature / total accounts in segment) * 100

known_variants.definition_delta: Uses active users (e.g., % of licensed users logging in weekly) instead of accounts for adoption metric
known_variants.when_to_use: Tracking engagement in usage-heavy products at user level

pitfalls: Definition of 'adopted' must be clear (e.g., feature used at least once vs used regularly)
Segments with few customers can show volatile percentages – consider raw counts too
High adoption in a segment doesn't automatically cause expansion – ensure to correlate but not assume causation

metric_definitions.definition: Average expansion (upsell/cross-sell) revenue per customer in a given segment or cohort, indicating propensity of that segment to grow after initial sale.
metric_definitions.formula: Expansion ARR per customer = Total expansion ARR from segment / # of customers in segment

known_variants.definition_delta: Expansion ARR in period divided by starting ARR of segment * 100 (growth rate from existing customers)
known_variants.when_to_use: To normalize expansion performance by segment size

pitfalls: Averages can be skewed by one large customer expansion in a small segment – consider median or distribution as well
Segments with low initial ARR can have high % expansion that isn't large in absolute terms
Ensure to exclude brand-new customers in measuring expansion (since they couldn't expand yet)

required_fields.description: E.g., did account use Feature X?

joins_and_keys.notes: Aggregate product usage metrics at account and segment level
Link expansion opportunities to account to sum expansion ARR by segment

transformation_rules: Define 'adopted' threshold for feature usage (e.g., performed key action X times within first 30 days)
Calculate adoption for cohorts if needed (e.g., customers onboarded in last 6 months) and compare segments
Only consider expansion deals from existing customers (exclude new business in expansion metric)

data_quality_checks.logic: Ensure product usage logging covers >95% of active accounts (no large gaps in tracking)
Accounts have a consistent segment classification between CRM and product analytics data

derived_fields: Adopted_Flag_per_account (1 if usage exceeds threshold, else 0)
Expansion_ARR_per_account (sum of upsells for that account in period)
aggregation_rules: Calculate segment adoption% = avg(Adopted_Flag) for accounts in segment
Sum expansion ARR by segment and divide by # of customers for average (or by starting ARR for expansion rate)
calculations_and_models.cohort_logic: Optional: group customers by start date (cohort) and track adoption speed and expansion over time by cohort within each segment
calculations_and_models.forecast_logic: Use adoption trends and historical expansion correlations to predict future expansion bookings (e.g., if X% adopt feature, expect Y expansion $ in segment)

layout.purpose: Compare what percentage of customers in each segment have adopted key features
drilldowns: time since onboarding (to see speed of adoption)

layout.purpose: Relate product adoption levels to expansion revenue for segments

narrative_insights_to_generate: Highlight segments with high adoption but low expansion (untapped upsell opportunities) and vice versa
Identify whether faster product adoption correlates with higher expansion revenue
diagnostic_questions: Which segment struggles to adopt the product and might need better onboarding?
Are our enterprise clients expanding more (in $) than SMB, even if SMB show higher adoption % of features?

recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Adoption rate in a segment significantly lags others (indicating engagement issues)

recommended_actions.action: Launch expansion campaigns targeting high-adoption customers
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Segment shows strong adoption but modest expansion sales (indicating room to upsell more modules)

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Basic chart of percent of customers using key feature by segment, and maybe a manual correlation observation with expansion sales
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Automated linkage between usage data and revenue outcomes, possibly with predictive scoring for which accounts are likely expansion candidates based on adoption patterns
common_client_customizations: Multiple adoption metrics (e.g., login frequency, feature X usage, license utilization) with ability to toggle
Include customer satisfaction or NPS by segment to add context to adoption and expansion willingness
privacy_and_access_notes: Usage data is fine for internal use; if shared externally (case studies), aggregate appropriately
Be mindful that low adoption segments might reflect product-market fit issues – sensitive in executive readouts
success_criteria: Segments with low adoption are targeted with specific initiatives and show subsequent adoption improvement
A measurable uptick in expansion sales in segments with previously untapped high adoption (closing the gap identified in this report)
